title: Repo Pairs API Routes
content:
This file defines the API routes for managing "repo pairs" within the DocMed application. Repo pairs likely represent linked repositories used for documentation generation or synchronization.

## Purpose

- Handles `DELETE` requests to remove a repo pair.
- Handles `PATCH` requests to update the name of an existing repo pair.

## Key Exports/Functions

- **`DELETE /app/api/repo-pairs/[pairId]`**
  - Authenticates the user using Clerk.
  - Retrieves the target repository details using `getTargetRepoFull`.
  - If a webhook is associated with the repo pair, it attempts to delete the GitHub webhook.
  - Deletes the repo pair from the database using `deleteRepoPair`.
  - Returns a `204 No Content` status upon successful deletion.

- **`PATCH /app/api/repo-pairs/[pairId]`**
  - Authenticates the user using Clerk.
  - Parses the request body to get the new name for the repo pair.
  - Validates that a `name` is provided.
  - Updates the repo pair's name in the database using `updateRepoPairName`.
  - Returns a `200 OK` status with `{ ok: true }` upon successful update.

## Usage Example

**Deleting a Repo Pair:**

```bash
curl -X DELETE https://your-app-url.com/app/api/repo-pairs/some-pair-id
```

**Updating a Repo Pair's Name:**

```bash
curl -X PATCH https://your-app-url.com/app/api/repo-pairs/some-pair-id \
     -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
     -d '{"name": "New Repo Pair Name"}'
```
